title: Sensitive Port 21 Open — FTP
description: Port 21/tcp (ftp) is publicly accessible on acme.com. FTP — cleartext file transfer. Sensitive services exposed to the internet are prime targets for brute-force, exploitation, and lateral movement.
action: Immediately restrict port 21 to internal network or VPN only via firewall rules. If this service is not needed, disable it entirely.

title: Sensitive Port 25 Open — SMTP
description: Port 25/tcp (smtp) is publicly accessible on acme.com. SMTP — mail relay. Sensitive services exposed to the internet are prime targets for brute-force, exploitation, and lateral movement.
action: Immediately restrict port 25 to internal network or VPN only via firewall rules. If this service is not needed, disable it entirely.

title: Open Directory Listing on Web Server — /.well-known/
description: Directory listing is enabled at http://acme.com/.well-known/ on the web server. Files in this directory are publicly browsable. Sample files: /.well-known%2f./, /.well-known%2f../, /.well-known%2fwebfinger.
action: Disable directory listing in your web server configuration (Apache: Options -Indexes; Nginx: remove autoindex on). Audit the directory for sensitive files.

title: Open Directory Listing on Web Server — /tmp/
description: Directory listing is enabled at http://acme.com/tmp/ on the web server. Files in this directory are publicly browsable.
action: Disable directory listing in your web server configuration (Apache: Options -Indexes; Nginx: remove autoindex on). Audit the directory for sensitive files.

title: Port 80 Open — HTTP
